project.name: Autonomous AI Scientist (MVP)
project.scope: Single-paper pipeline + multi-agent hypothesis generation + critic + traceability

claims.claim_text: We introduce the Hierarchical Seating Allocation Problem (HSAP) which addresses the optimal assignment of hier-archically structured organizational teams to physical seat-ing arrangements on a floor plan.
claims.evidence_text: We introduce the Hierarchical Seating Allocation Problem (HSAP) which addresses the optimal assignment of hier-archically structured organizational teams to physical seat-ing arrangements on a floor plan.

claims.claim_text: To alleviate this manual process, we propose an end-to-end framework to solve the HSAP.
claims.evidence_text: To alleviate this manual process, we propose an end-to-end framework to solve the HSAP.

claims.claim_text: We demonstrate our approach under dif-ferent sized instances by evaluating the PRM framework and subsequent allocations both quantitatively and qualitatively.
claims.evidence_text: We demonstrate our approach under dif-ferent sized instances by evaluating the PRM framework and subsequent allocations both quantitatively and qualitatively.

gaps_actionable.gap_statement: Increase robustness to floor-plan quality: handle noisy/low-quality plans without extensive preprocessing.
gaps_actionable.evidence_text: A key challenge in this domain is the Seating Allocation (SA) problem, which involves assigning teams to office space on a floor plan.

gaps_actionable.gap_statement: Improve alignment with human preferences: incorporate preference-aware objectives or constraints.
gaps_actionable.evidence_text: The usefulness between HSA and SA is significant to large organizations as the humanevaluated solution quality to SA would be low due to failure to accommodate linked departments.

gaps_actionable.gap_statement: Improve scalability: reduce computational complexity / variables so the approach works for large instances.
gaps_actionable.evidence_text: A direct optimization approach could be employed to minimize the total seat distance between each seat, but this results in a mixed-integer quadratic problem (MIQP), which is not scalable for large instances due to the number of decision variables in the objective.

gaps_actionable.gap_statement: Improve alignment with human preferences: incorporate preference-aware objectives or constraints.
gaps_actionable.evidence_text: Conversely, if the allocation framework in step 2 does not adequately model human preferences, the solution quality, as reviewed by a human, would similarly be poor.

gaps_actionable.gap_statement: Reduce manual effort: automate tuning or reduce reliance on manual editing/parameter selection.
gaps_actionable.evidence_text: For high resolution floor plans when projected into x, y-plane, obtaining such efficient representations may require substantial manual editing.

gaps_actionable.gap_statement: Increase robustness to floor-plan quality: handle noisy/low-quality plans without extensive preprocessing.
gaps_actionable.evidence_text: Similarly, finding an adequate representation is also made difficult if the quality of the provided floor plans is low.

gaps_actionable.gap_statement: Reduce manual effort: automate tuning or reduce reliance on manual editing/parameter selection.
gaps_actionable.evidence_text: Moreover, for large organizations with extensive office footprints, manually adjusting each representation to compute optimal pairwise seat distances may be infeasible.

gaps_actionable.gap_statement: Improve robustness: identify failure modes and prevent repeated trial-and-error runs.
gaps_actionable.evidence_text: If the algorithm fails to connect all the seats, K can be increased, and the process repeated.

gaps_actionable.gap_statement: Improve scalability: reduce computational complexity / variables so the approach works for large instances.
gaps_actionable.evidence_text: The IPSA approach is computational expensive in practice due to the rapidly increasing number of variables in xik (|S|2), which makes large-scale problems intractable.

gaps_actionable.gap_statement: Reduce manual effort: automate tuning or reduce reliance on manual editing/parameter selection.
gaps_actionable.evidence_text: The hyper-parameters for δc and δs were manually tuned until the algorithm converged with a node limit of K = 2000.

gaps_actionable.gap_statement: Improve scalability: reduce computational complexity / variables so the approach works for large instances.
gaps_actionable.evidence_text: Solving the HSAP problem as a single monolithic instance is intractable for small, medium and large scale instances (i.e. over 50 seats).

gaps_actionable.gap_statement: Refine and specify this gap into a testable research question.
gaps_actionable.evidence_text: Empirically, varying |S| or |T| across different instances is insufficient to gauge the time-to-solution, as the difficulty and structure of the organizational hierarchy can vary unpredictably.

gaps_actionable.gap_statement: Improve alignment with human preferences: incorporate preference-aware objectives or constraints.
gaps_actionable.evidence_text: Although the optimization procedure was solved to optimality, a human decision-maker might choose to manually relocate the two teal seats in the top right quadrant to be adjacent, even if this adjustment slightly reduces the overall optimization objective.

gaps_actionable.gap_statement: Increase robustness to floor-plan quality: handle noisy/low-quality plans without extensive preprocessing.
gaps_actionable.evidence_text: The ball-shaped output from using PRM may not be ideal for open and densely packed office floor plans.

gaps_actionable.gap_statement: Improve alignment with human preferences: incorporate preference-aware objectives or constraints.
gaps_actionable.evidence_text: Future work should try to improve the disconnect between the constrained programming and human-evaluated solution quality by studying in which scenarios different solution methods and distance estimation techniques performs best.

gaps_actionable.gap_statement: Investigate recommended future direction described by the authors and propose a concrete experiment to test it.
gaps_actionable.evidence_text: Future work could focus on developing a dynamic pipeline to determine the most suitable algorithm(s) based on the size and complexity of each subproblem could be beneficial.

hypothesis_lineage.hypothesis_text: Using a robust floor-plan preprocessing pipeline (noise removal + wall detection confidence thresholds) will reduce distance-estimation errors on low-quality floor plans.
hypothesis_lineage.failure_mode: If distance estimation error remains high on low-quality plans, the hypothesis is false.

critic.suggestion: Feasibility: start with 3–5 toy floor plans; compare PRM/RRT distance error vs Euclidean/Manhattan as a proxy.

reflection.why: Kept because it meets rubric thresholds with a strong total score.
reflection.improvement_plan: Feasibility: start with 3–5 toy floor plans; compare PRM/RRT distance error vs Euclidean/Manhattan as a proxy.

trace.evidence_text: A key challenge in this domain is the Seating Allocation (SA) problem, which involves assigning teams to office space on a floor plan.

gap.gap_statement: Increase robustness to floor-plan quality: handle noisy/low-quality plans without extensive preprocessing.
gap.evidence_text: A key challenge in this domain is the Seating Allocation (SA) problem, which involves assigning teams to office space on a floor plan.

hypothesis_lineage.hypothesis_text: Adding a preference-aware term (e.g., contiguity/adjacency penalties) to the objective will improve human-rated seating quality without large optimization cost.
hypothesis_lineage.failure_mode: If human ratings do not improve or optimization cost increases too much, the hypothesis is false.

critic.suggestion: Make it more testable: specify metric(s), baseline(s), and what counts as improvement.

reflection.why: Kept because it meets rubric thresholds with a strong total score.
reflection.improvement_plan: Make it more testable: specify metric(s), baseline(s), and what counts as improvement.

gap.evidence_text: The usefulness between HSA and SA is significant to large organizations as the humanevaluated solution quality to SA would be low due to failure to accommodate linked departments.

hypothesis_lineage.hypothesis_text: Replacing the quadratic-variable formulation with a decomposition + heuristic warm-start will reduce runtime while preserving allocation quality on large instances.
hypothesis_lineage.failure_mode: If runtime does not decrease on large instances or allocation quality drops significantly versus baseline, the hypothesis is false.

critic.suggestion: Feasibility: test on synthetic instances varying |S| and |T|; report runtime + quality vs IPSA baseline under a fixed time budget.

disagreement_log.evidence_text: A key challenge in this domain is the Seating Allocation (SA) problem, which involves assigning teams to office space on a floor plan.

agent_outputs.hypothesis_text: A noise-robust preprocessing pipeline (denoising + wall-confidence filtering) will reduce PRM/RRT distance-estimation errors on low-quality floor plans.

critic.suggestion: Add a lightweight proxy experiment to validate feasibility.

agent_outputs.hypothesis_text: If we ablate preprocessing steps (denoise, wall-detection thresholding), we will observe that distance-estimation error is dominated by wall-detection failures on noisy plans.

critic.suggestion: Add a lightweight proxy experiment to validate feasibility.

disagreement_summary.reason: Decision-aware preference (KEEP > REVISE > REJECT), then total score.

disagreement_log.evidence_text: The usefulness between HSA and SA is significant to large organizations as the humanevaluated solution quality to SA would be low due to failure to accommodate linked departments.

agent_outputs.hypothesis_text: Adding an adjacency/contiguity penalty to the optimization objective will improve human-rated seating quality without significantly increasing runtime.

critic.suggestion: Add a lightweight proxy experiment to validate feasibility.

agent_outputs.hypothesis_text: A two-stage approach (optimize constraints first, then apply a local human-preference refinement) will yield higher human satisfaction than a single-objective solver.
